entry.title: Re: COHV Availability Check clears MSPT but does not upda...
entry.content:
Hello @darien 

Your scenario steps don't contain any reference to COHV availability check. Thus, it's all but impossible if or when you execute availability check.

In general, availability check of production order components should be done before release, to make sure you have enough components to complete the order. It looks like you don't follow that - you have COGI for unavailable components and you reprocess COGI when the components are available. I don't see where COHV availability check sits in your scenario.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Standard API to Maintain Dunning Level and Last Dunned During AR-to-AR Reclassification
entry.content:
We are performing an AR-to-AR reclassification in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

We currently use either:

  • the Journal Entry - Post (Asynchronous) API, or
  • Upload Customer Open Items,

However, neither option allows us to provide or preserve the following dunning information:

  • Dunning Level
  • Last Dunned

These fields can be maintained manually in the Manage Customer Line Items app, but for a large number of documents this is not feasible.

Could you please confirm whether there is any standard API, import object, mass upload functionality, or any other supported interface that allows us to create customer open items including the Dunning Level and Last Dunned fields?

Our goal is to preserve the dunning status during an AR-to-AR reclassification without requiring manual maintenance after the posting.

entry.title: How to built rule for leave eligibility using a rolling 12-month period from first leave applied?
entry.content:
Business Requirement

Leave Type: Unpaid Leave

Entitlement: The maximum number of leave days is determined by the employee's assigned work schedule (working days per week).

  • Example:
    • 3 working days/week = 3 unpaid leave days per rolling 12 months.
    • 5 working days/week = 5 unpaid leave days per rolling 12 months.

Evaluation Period:

  • Rolling 12-month period starting from the employee's first approved Unpaid Leave request, not a calendar year or fixed leave year.
  • The first approved leave request establishes the start of the employee's personal 12-month window.
  • All subsequent Unpaid Leave requests within that 12-month window must be validated against the employee's maximum entitlement.
  • If the cumulative approved leave days within that rolling period exceed the employee's eligible limit, the leave request should be rejected with an appropriate error message.
  • Once the 12-month period expires, a new 12-month window begins based on the next approved leave request.

Example:

 

  • Employee work schedule = 3 working days/week.
  • Maximum Unpaid Leave entitlement = 3 days.
  • First approved Unpaid Leave = 12 July 2026.
  • Rolling period = 12 July 2026 to 11 July 2027.
  • Employee can take a total of 3 days during this period.
  • If another request causes the total to exceed 3 days, the system should reject the request.
  • After 11 July 2027, the next approved Unpaid Leave starts a new rolling 12-month period with a fresh entitlement of 3 days.

I would appreciate any help or insights regarding the requirement above. If you have any suggestions, recommendations, or see any potential issues or considerations, please share your thoughts.

Thank you in advance for your time and support.

Best Regards,

Venkat

entry.title: Re: Learning and Certification for the AI Era: SAP Completes the Transformation of Certification
entry.content:
@DianaRoesner I appreciate your posting on the impact of AI in the Learning and Upskilling processes. I would say that it is really mind blowing how AI has made learning much easier with the inclusion of Hands-on Practical Assessments to theoretical studies of SAP skill programs.

As an SAP learner, I would want to share my own learning experience. I would say that I am much more fired up to do more with the hands-on practical assessments than I am with the theoretical notes and quiz questions. To analyze myself that I am more of a practical kind of person, I would say Yes, I am, but to say that I am not a theoretical kind of person, I would disagree. And this is because I have studied and achieved other certifications programs for my professional upskilling. What I discover is that there are a lot of repetitions of knowledge information in multiple paragraphs under the same knowledge topic. There is no human reflection in the notes that are written. I read and immediately loose connection in what I am reading because in my mind, I would say I just finished reading this, why is it repeated again?

Could this be a flaw in AI, where we say it is hallucinating? How do we navigate this AI era of change, where they are so much information but still looks chaotic?

entry.title: Re: Whether the SAP EWM module supports type B-Customer S...
entry.content:
Hello @s0026242023 

Check the note 3197261 - Customer Stocks in Extended Warehouse Management - Additional Functionality. Check if the note is valid for your system.

Then check the notes:

  • 3226690 - Customer Stocks in EWM - Setup Control Parameters for ERP Version Control
  • 3226687 - Customer Stocks in EWM - Allow Creation of Deliveries in Logistics Execution

As per those notes you need to allow customer stock management in SPRO:  Logistics Execution -> Extended Warehouse Management Integration -> Basic Setup of Connectivity -> Configure SAP EWM-Specific Parameters::

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Making AI Real: Promocean
entry.content:
When it comes to AI, there are so many possibilities that it can be hard to know where to start. And that’s why real-world examples of AI in production are so valuable. Here’s a great one from the 2026 SAP Innovation Awards: Promocean, who managed to get value from AI in their existing ECC environment, while future-proofing a move to S/4HANA at some point.

Imagine you’re a world-class brand like Heineken, Lindt chocolates, Bacardi, or Lacoste, and you’re organizing a promotional event. You want the event to feel special, different, creative, but still 100% aligned with your brand image — and of course for the right price, delivered on time. That’s when you might turn to Promocean, the winner of the Technology Pathfinder category of the 2026 SAP Innovation Awards. 

The Dutch company is trusted by some of the top brands in the world to create award-winning point of sale marketing materials and packaging. 

Procurement is crucial for Promocean because they need to efficiently source very customized materials and services from around the world, while meeting strict cost, quality, and delivery requirements. 

Surveys show that communication between vendors and buyers is a major source of frustration in almost every industry. Promocean had an overloaded team spending up to 19 hours a day on purchase order follow-ups, using manual processes based on email and excel that just didn’t scale. 

In addition, supply chain delays weren’t as visible as they should be, there was no easy way to track the reliability of different vendors. and when issues did come up, it was hard to collaborate across different internal teams.

At a business AI hackathon organized late last year by SAP Netherlands and the Dutch SAP user group, VNSG, Promocean saw an opportunity to use AI to reduce these risks and inefficiencies. Working with their long-term strategic consulting partner SOA PEOPLE and an SAP expert coach, they designed and implemented an AI-driven procurement solution powered by SAP Business AI Platform. 

The way it works is interesting. First, there’s the design phase using AI. Promocean had a big advantage — they had already thoroughly documented what the process should look like. Then using the SAP AI platform, they fed that into a GPT model using a now-standard RAG approach, retrieval augmented generation, and used it to create a very sophisticated and complex rules-based engine for handling different types of enquiries. 

Then there’s the operational phase: an unattended bot using SAP Build Process Automation gets emails from Outlook every 30 minutes. Those are then interpreted using large language models from Mistral and Anthropic on SAP’s GenAI Hub and enriched with back-end data from an on-premise SAP ECC system — for example looking up the current status of an order or invoice.

The email summary is then passed onto the rules engine to determine the best next step, and generate intelligent action proposals. Users can then review and manage these proposals in real-time, using the very latest information available, using a custom Purchase Order Control Center built with Fiori and the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model in SAP Build Work Zone.

The final choice is always with the end-user. If they accept the action, the next steps can be carried out automatically by the system. If they disagree with the proposed solution, that feedback is fed back into the system to improve it over time. 

The solution went live on January 19th 2026 after less than 20 weeks of development, resulting in more automated, timely, and consistent follow-up of purchase orders — reducing manual effort by 63% and with fewer errors. The team estimates that by leveraging AI techniques they were able to reduce development effort by 90%! There’s faster and more consistent vendor follow-up as the team is able to move from repetitive operational work to focus on what’s important.  And the system is scalable and future-proof, a platform for other AI projects, providing value today while being ready for Promocean’s move to S/4 HANA at some point in the future. 

Lessons learned:

  • Look for opportunities to participate in hackathons and design workshops with your strategic vendors and partners, to find the best. most feasible AI projects for your organization, and make sure you involve the employees concerned, to tap into their knowledge and excitement.
  • Real-world AI works best when it’s built on top of a robust foundation, like SAP Business AI platform, that helps you move from prototype to production, and prepare for the AI future.

You can get more details about the solution in Promocean’s SAP Innovation Award entry and inin this video from Twan van den Broek:

At a business AI hackathon organized late last year by SAP Netherlands and the Dutch SAP user group, VNSG, Promocean saw an opportunity to use AI to reduce these risks and inefficiencies. Working with their long-term strategic consulting partner SOA PEOPLE and an SAP expert coach, they designed and implemented an AI-driven procurement solution powered by SAP Business AI Platform.

entry.title: Bulk Upload of Inspection Results in SAP
entry.content:
We need a way to upload inspection results for serialized materials in bulk in SAP.

The standard "Record Inspection Results in Table form" app allows users to record inspection results, but it does not support uploading results for multiple inspection lots from an Excel file. Therefore, users must enter the inspection results manually for each inspection lot.

Each inspection lot contains 20 to 50 serial numbers. Even when the inspection result is the same for all serial numbers, users must enter the same result separately for each serial number. This makes the process slow and time-consuming.

Is there a standard SAP solution to upload inspection results in bulk from an Excel file? If not, is custom development required? If custom development is needed, please provide the recommended approach and the step-by-step process.

entry.title: Re: Mastering SAP Datasphere Replication Scheduling
entry.content:
@nilrod do you mean events in S/4? or in other systems? It is now possible to schedule via API Managing Tasks via the REST API 

What I'm really missing is a way to schedule depending on plant calendars like you can do in S/4. E.g. load more frequent the first 4 workdays of a month.

@yogen_patil I think that you miss quite a lot by only looking at the technical differences here, they kind of doesn't matter that much. It's much more important to understand the differences between "Frequency-Based Scheduling" and the Scheduled runs in terms of in which context they can be used and which limitations it gives.

Frequency based is more mature technically e.g. it allows you to stop a replication flow (which will clean up on the source side). If you choose to schedule (simple or cron) then you can't stop a replication flow. The frequency is a number of hours / minutes / days after the last job terminates i.e. there is no risk of overlapping loads.

Scheduled jobs on the other hand can be started in task chains and you can thereby start related jobs when the replication has finished, which is not possible when using frequency based schedules. On the negative side you can start replication flows that are all ready running which will cause your chain to fail, i.e. you will have to consider more error handling.

With kind regards

Kristian

entry.title: What is SAP Joule? SAP's AI Copilot Explained for SAP Professionals
entry.content:
Meet SAP Joule: SAP's AI Copilot for the Intelligent Enterprise

Artificial Intelligence is becoming an integral part of enterprise software, and SAP has introduced its own AI copilot called SAP Joule to help users work smarter and faster.

Unlike traditional chatbots, SAP Joule is deeply integrated into SAP applications and understands business processes, business data, and user context. Its goal is to reduce manual effort and help users make better decisions without constantly switching between multiple screens or reports.

What Can SAP Joule Do?

Some of its key capabilities include:

  • Generate business insights using natural language.
  • Retrieve information across SAP applications.
  • Assist with business tasks and workflows.
  • Summarize reports and business data.
  • Support finance, procurement, HR, supply chain, and other business functions.
  • Help users complete routine activities more efficiently.

Why Does It Matter?

Organizations are generating more business data than ever before. Finding the right information at the right time can be challenging.

SAP Joule brings AI directly into everyday business operations, enabling users to:

  • Improve productivity.
  • Reduce manual work.
  • Access insights faster.
  • Make more informed decisions.
  • Enhance the overall SAP user experience.

Looking Ahead

SAP continues to expand Joule's capabilities across its portfolio, making AI an increasingly important part of modern enterprise applications. As more organizations adopt intelligent ERP solutions, understanding tools like SAP Joule will become valuable for SAP professionals, consultants, and business users alike.

Have you had a chance to explore SAP Joule? I'd be interested to hear your thoughts and use cases in the comments.

Follow me for more SAP updates, product insights, and upcoming innovations.

entry.title: Bulk Managing SAP HCM Payroll Data
entry.content:
Business Case: Mass Updates in SAP HCM Payroll

Before choosing a tool, it is worth understanding your architecture. SAP customers running SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central (EC) integrated with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) have two fundamentally different paths for loading employee data in bulk.

Option A - SAP SuccessFactors Data Import/Export (Recommended)

The cleanest approach is to use the built-in Data Import/Export feature inside SAP SuccessFactors. Data entered here is the system of record in EC, and it replicates automatically to ECP via the standard integration. This means:

  • Data is consistent between EC and ECP at all times.
  • The employee sees their own data accurately in the self-service people profile.
  • Audit trails and approvals flow through the normal SuccessFactors workflow.

For most organizations, this is the right approach for any data  that employees can view or that HR needs to report on across the full HR suite.

Option B - Direct ECP Tools (LSMW, PA70, HRFE_MASS_UPDATE)

For data that is purely operational payroll data - managed exclusively by the Payroll Administrator, invisible to employees, and with no corresponding object in EC - loading directly into ECP via a transaction-based tool is acceptable. Common examples include:

  • Irregular income wage types (IT0015) for internal allowance schemes
  • Recurring income (IT0014) for position allowance
  • One-time statutory contributions manage by payroll only.

Note: Going direct into ECP bypasses EC entirely. The data will not appear in SuccessFactors EC, is not subject to EC workflows, and creates no EC record. Use this path only when those conditions are acceptable.

With that context established, let us look at the three main tools available for direct ECP mass update.

Tool Comparison: PA70, LSMW, and HRFE_MASS_UPDATE

SAP provides three well-known tools for bulk infotype management. Each has a specific sweet spot:

CriterionPA70 (Fast Entry)Legacy System Migration Workbench (LSMW)HRFE_MASS_UPDATE
Primary UseQuick manual entry for small groupLegacy data migration; one time large loadsRecurring operational mass updates
Volume LimitVisible Screen limit (20 records), limit 999 records per sessionNo limitNo limit
Input MethodManual, on-screen onlyFile upload with field mappingFile upload or online entry
Rollback/Undo❌None built-in❌Complex re-run required✅Instant, atomic
Background Job⚠️Limited✅Yes✅Yes
Setup complexityLowHigh (consultant required)Medium
Who runs itHR AdministratorIT/Functional ConsultantHR Administrator

 Reference: SAP Help Portal - HRFE_MASS_UPDATE

Technical prerequisite for HRFE_MASS_UPDATE: The PC_UI indicator in table T77S0 (group CCURE) must be active. This switch is set once during system setup by the Basis/Configuration team. Without it, HRFE_MASS_UPDATE will not function.

When to Use Which Tool

Use PA70 when: An HR admin needs to quickly update data for 5-20 employees and does not have time to prepare file. Ideal for truly small, one-off, manual tasks.

Use LSMW when: You are migrating historical data from a legacy system before go-live, or running a one-time annual load where the IT team owns the process. LSMW's flexibility handles scenario HRFE_MASS_UPDATE cannot, such as creating HR Actions or updating infotypes not yet supported for mass update.

Use HRFE_MASS_UPDATE when: You have a recurring operational process (monthly allowance, quarterly bonus IT0015 records, periodic allowance loads) where the HR team owns execution and you need a reliable rollback mechanism. This is the tool purpose-built for payroll operations teams.

The Real Challenge: What Happens When You Need to Undo a Batch?

Imagine this common scenario: Your Payroll Administrator loads irregular income data (IT0015) for 800 employees - Travel allowance, Meal Allowances, or any income that employees receive irregularly. These are uploaded in batch lots of 100, 200, or 1,000 records at a time.

After uploading, the team realizes that one specific batch lot was imported with the wrong amount - perhaps the wrong wage type was used, or the date period is off by one month. The question becomes: 

🤔 The key question: Can we filter the records from that specific batch lot and delete only those records - without touching any other records uploaded before or after?

With standard SAP tools used in their default configuration, the answer is: not easily. There is no built-in batch identifier on IT0015 records. You would need to manually identify each record by employee number, date, and wage type - a slow and error-prone process for large volumes.

The solution lies in a concept we call the Batch ID Mechanism - and it can be implemented entirely with standard SAP fields, no custom development required.

The Batch ID Mechanism Explained

The Batch ID Mechanism is a simple operational pattern built on two standard SAP fields that already exist on IT0015 (Additional Payments): Assignment Number and Record Sequence Number

SAP FieldRole in Batch ID Mechanism
Assignment NumberActs as the Batch ID - you populate this with a unique key (e.g. 'XX9999') shared by every record in the same upload batch.
Record Sequence NumberA system-generated key that uniquely identifies each IT0015 record. Used as the deletion key when you want to remove specific records.

How It Works - Three Step

1. Tag the batch. Generate your own Batch ID and populate the Assignment Number field - use a consistent, unique value (e.g. 'XX9999') for all records in the same upload batch. This is your batch fingerprint.

2. Find the records. After upload, use a query tool (e.g., SE16 on table PA0015, or SAP Query SQ01, SQ02 and SQ03 to generate report) to retrieve all records that share the Batch ID (Assignment Number = 'XX9999'). Note the Record Sequence Number for each record.

3. Delete the batch. Run HRFE_MASS_UPDATE with Operation = Delete, filtering by the Batch ID. All records tagged with that Batch ID are removed atomically, leaving every other batch untouched.

Visual: Before and After Batch Delete

🤓Analogy: This pattern is conceptually similar to a database transaction rollback - commit a batch, receive a token, use the token to undo. The difference is that here, you create the token yourself by populating the Assignment Number field before the upload.

Using HRFE_MASS_UPDATE: Step by Step

HRFE_MASS_UPDATE (introduced by SAP Note 3426157) is the recommended tool for the Batch ID workflow. The transaction is supported for HCM Localization infotypes (SAP Note 3474709) and all International infotypes (SAP Note 3504532).

The transaction walks through five steps.

Step 1 - Define Infotype, Subtype, and Time Period

Specify which infotype you are updating and foe which date range. 

- Infotype Version 26 applies Thailand country-specific and validations

- Leave End date blank to create open-ended records.

Step 2 - Choose Save Option

  • Save records directly - writes to the database immediately. User for smaller volume or when instant feedback is needed.
  • Create Job - schedules a background batch job. Recommended for uploads affecting 1,000+ employees to avoid dialog work process timeouts.

Best Practice: For monthly allowance uploads at scale, always use Create Job and schedule it for off-peak hours to minimise locking contention.

Step 3 - Choose Employee Selection Method

Four options are available:

Selection MethodBest For
Upload or enter together with infotype dataMost common: upload an Excel/CSV containing both personal number and field values.
Upload or manual preselectionManually type a list of personnel numbers, then enter data on-screen.
Preselection with Ad Hoc QueryRun a quick ad hoc query to dynamically select employees (e.g., all employees in a cost center).
Preselection using ReportLink a pre-built SAP Query report as the selection source - no programming required.

Step 4 - Choose Operation

OperationDescription
CreateCreates new infotype records as specified. Fails if a conflicting record already exists.
Create with proposalShows a preview of each record before committing - use this for the first run of any new template.
ChangeModifies fields in existing records. Option to restrict to locked records only.
DeleteRemoves existing records. Filter by Batch ID (Assignment Number) for targeted deletion.
Lock Locks records to prevent payroll from picking them up during review.
UnlockRelease previously locked records.

Tip: Always use Create with proposal on the first run of any new upload template. It lets you catch field-mapping errors before writing anything to the database.

Step 5 - Upload Data, Execute, and Review Results

For file based uploads, drag and drop or paste your personnel numbers and data into the entry screen. After execution, the result list shows every record processed:

Note the Assignment Number column (BatchID = XX9999). This is your rollback key for the entire batch.

Real-World Example: Monthly Allowance Load

Company processes thousand of allowance IT0015 records every month. Here is how the Batch ID pattern supports their operational workflow:

Step 1:  Select Create with Proposal to prepare Assignment Number data (or Batch ID) on screen record and maintain data  or prepare the monthly data on the Excel template from HRFE_MASS_UPDATE to upload.

By selecting Create with Proposal Option, it shows the template screen to maintain the default data for all records. 

The Assignment Number of all records is default with the value from the proposal.

On the Create screen click Export button to download Excel template. After maintain value completely, click Import button to update data on the screen and click save to create records on IT0015.

 

 

 

 

Step 2: Payroll Verification. The payroll team reviews the imported records. Because all records share the same Batch Id, any count or total discrepancy is immediately traceable to the specific batch.

 

Step 3: Correction if needed. If an error is found: provide the Batch ID -> run the query report by using Batch ID on the assignment number as a key to find the list and get the Record Sequence Number for each. In this example, I created the SAP Query report to list out the IT0015 data.

The RNo column is a required record key for the Delete operation in case employee has the record on the same date more than one. The RNo column will help define the record to delete it correctly. Run the query by using the Assignment Number as a key to filter the record for the deletion.

Export data from the report in the spreadsheet format that we can use to import it into HRFE_MASS_UPDATE for the delete operation.

 

Step 4 Run HRFE_MASS_UPDATE operation = Delete filtering by import the file with Record Sequence Number value that downloaded from the query report. --> all affected record are removed in one shot.

After import the file successfully, the data shows on the screen and ready to take the Delete action by clicking Save button. The system will show the message delete successfully, the data in IT0015 is deleted.

Summary and Quick Reference

The Batch ID Mechanism require no custom ABAP development. It is built entirely on two standard SAP fields and standard transaction operations. The key insight is:

  • Assignment Number = your self-managed Batch ID key.
  • Record Sequence Number = the system's deletion key.
  • HRFE_MASS_UPDATE Delete operation = the rollback mechanism.
  • The combination gives payroll operations a reversible, auditable bulk data process.

SAP Note Reference: 

  • 3426157 HRFE_MASS_UPDATE - new functionality and prerequisites
  • 3474709 HCM localization infotypes supported in mass update
  • 3504532 International Infotypes supported in mass update

Closing Thoughts

The Batch ID Mechanism is a simple idea, but it changes the operation for payroll administrators working with high-volume infotype data. What used to require IT intervention and careful manual searching can now be handled end-to-end by the HR team - faster, safer, and with full auditability.

entry.title: National Transfer in SAP ECP: Same PERNR vs New PERNR Decision Guide
entry.content:
The Transfer Question That Trips Up Every ECP Implementation

When an employee moves between legal entities in the same country, a single technical question drives the entire HR configuration: same Personnel Number (PERNR), or new one?

It sounds simple. It is not. The answer touches payroll continuity, data protection, statutory reporting, and a BAdI that most implementations have not yet addressed. This document walks through the decision, the SAP-confirmed technical path, and the authorization design — based on SAP Notes 3124029 and 1902797, SAP Help Portal (1H 2026), and live project experience.

Two Scenarios — One Decision

Scenario A — Same PERNR

The Job Information portlet on the existing Employee Central (EC) employment is updated to the new company. No new employment is created, no new User ID is assigned, and no new PERNR is generated in ECP. The employee's payroll history, year-to-date (YTD) results, and infotype records remain on a single personnel number.

Scenario B — New PERNR

The old employment is terminated and a new employment is created in EC. ECP terminates the old PERNR and hires a new one. YTD payroll results do not transfer. Manual entry and custom development are required.

SAP Default Behavior

SAP's default is Scenario B. Every time a new company code (BUKRS) appears in the EC-to-ECP replication, the system checks the Employee Key Mapping table (HRSFEC_D_EEKEYMP) and — if no PERNR is mapped for that company code — creates a new one. Even if the EC side keeps the same employment, ECP generates a Termination + Hiring action pair automatically.

Tax result accumulation is another key consideration. With a new employment, the YTD  tax result from previous company must be manually entered as opening balances on the new PERNR before the first payroll run - there is no standard SAP program to carry them across. With the same employment, tax result accumulation continues uninterrupted on a single record.

Figure 1: Manage Employments across the SuccessFactors suites National Transfer — Decision guide for same employment or new employment (SAP Employee Central, May 2024)

How to Achieve Same PERNR: The BAdI

Per SAP Note 3124029, same PERNR is only possible when all three of the following conditions are satisfied simultaneously:

ConditionRequirement

EC EmploymentSame employment - no new employment created
CountrySame country - cross country is not possible
Data TimingCompany change in EC is after the Full transmission Start Date (FTSD)

When all three are met, custom code is still required. BAdI HRSFEC_B_CE_DECIDE_HIRE_REHIRE must be implemented to prevent ECP from treating the company code change as a new hire. Without this BAdI, ECP creates a new PERNR regardless of the EC employment status.

Mandatory Technical Prerequisite No BAdI = No same PERNR. Implementing HRSFEC_B_CE_DECIDE_HIRE_REHIRE is the single mandatory prerequisite for Scenario A. This is a modification-free BAdI enhancement point.

The Mid-Month Wall

There is one hard constraint that no BAdI can remove: changing the payroll area in Infotype 0001 mid-payroll-period triggers RP 221 — Payroll area change possible only at the end of a payroll period. This is an ABAP kernel constraint documented in KBA 2536159 and is not configurable.

A cross-company transfer almost always changes the payroll area, since each legal entity typically has its own. The implications are clear:

  • Transfer at period boundary (month-end): Scenario A works.
  • Transfer mid-month: Scenario A is blocked. Scenario B must be used.

Practical Implication If the customer's HR policy allows transfers at any time of month, it is not possible to standardize on Scenario A for all cases. An exception process must be designed,or a business rule enforced that restricts transfers to the last day of the month.

Solving the Authorization Question

The most common objection to Scenario A same PERNR is: "The old company HR administrator will still be able to see our employee's data." This is a legitimate concern — but it is solvable without creating a new PERNR.

Figure 2: Move-date and Period of Responsibility — when an employee transfers between personal areas, the move-date marks the change of administrator responsibility (SAP AG, Time Logic in HR Authorizations)

T582A-VALDT: The Authorization Time Logic Setting

SAP's authorization time logic, documented in SAP Note 1902797, controls access via the V_T582A field T582A-VALDT:

  • SPACE:  VALDT = SPACE: The administrator is authorized if they were responsible for the employee even one day — no time restriction applies.
  • 'X':  VALDT = 'X': Time-dependent. Access is checked against the current system date relative to the transfer date (move-date).

 

 

Figure 3: T582A-VALDT logic — the system determines whether authorization is time-dependent (VALDT = 'X') or unrestricted (VALDT = SPACE) (SAP AG, Time Logic in HR Authorizations)

Access Matrix: VALDT = 'X' Active

Administrator

Period

Read

Write

Old company

Before move-date

Yes

Yes

Old company

After move-date

Yes (until tolerance expires)

No

Old company

After tolerance time

No

No

New company

Before move-date

Yes (historical read)

No

New company

From move-date onward

Yes

Yes

 

Figure 4: Tolerance Time (1) — old administrator retains write access until Move-date + T (T = AUTSW ADAYS in T77S0, default 15 days) (SAP AG, Time Logic in HR Authorizations)

Figure 5: Tolerance Time (2) — after tolerance window expires, old administrator loses all access to the employee's records (SAP AG, Time Logic in HR Authorizations)

Infotype Strategy for Clean Segregation

Not every infotype requires a new record on transfer date. The following approach achieves clean data segregation:

InfotypeAction RequiredRationale
IT0008 - Basic PayCreate new record on transfer date with new company salary structureNew record falls in new admin's responsibility period only
IT0014 - Additional PaymentsCreate new record on transfer dateSensitive compensation data; old admin must not see new values
IT0006 - AddressesNo change neededBoth companies share read access during overlap - operationally acceptable
IT0009 - Bank DetailsNo change neededBoth companies originally held this data.
IT0021 - Family/Related PersonNo change neededShared demographic data; dual read access is acceptable

For custom authorization logic, BAdIs HRPAD00AUTH_CHECK and HRPAD00AUTH_TIME are available as modification-free enhancement points (available since SAP 4.6C).

 Thailand Statutory Reporting — Status

Report

Same PERNR

Notes

Personal Income Tax 91 Form

Supported

Full-year income on one PERNR; run PC00_M26_CTX91 with PERNR + form authorization

Monthly Tax Form PND1 / Annual Tax Form PND1A

Supported

PNPCE database with time-dependent logic; Tax Payer ID (Company Code) partitions the records

Social Security Report SSO 1-03, 1-03/1, 6-09

Supported

SAP Note 3749752 — handles SSO ID/branch change under same PERNR; apply before go-live

Workmen's Compensation Report

Supported

Standard PNP/PNPCE time-dependent logic; partitioned by Tax Payer ID

Decision Guide

Before committing to a design, answer these four questions in sequence:

#

Question

Answer

Direction

1

Do employees ever transfer mid-month?

Yes

Scenario B required for those cases (RP 221 constraint)

2

Is the company change on the same EC employment, in the same country?

No to either

Scenario A is impossible by design

3

Is YTD payroll continuity important?

Yes

Scenario A strongly preferred. Scenario B needs custom YTD copy program — no SAP standard tool exists

4

Is strict old-company data isolation required?

Yes

Configure T582A-VALDT='X' per infotype and create new records for sensitive infotypes (IT0008, IT0014). New PERNR is rarely justified by authorization concerns alone

Summary

Keeping the same PERNR across a national cross-company transfer is technically achievable in SAP ECP. However, it requires four elements to be in place:

#RequirementDetail
1BAdI HRSFEC_B_CE_DECIDE_HIRE_REHIRECustom implementation to block the default new-hire action triggered by a company code change
2Transfer timingPeriod boundary only (month-end). Mid-month transfers are blocked by RP 221 — this constraint is not configurable
3Authorization designT582A-VALDT='X' with selective new infotype records on transfer date for sensitive compensation data
4Thailand SSO reportsSAP Note 3749752 applied before go-live to handle SSO ID/branch change under same PERNR

When these four elements are in place, Scenario A delivers automated YTD continuity, a single PIT91 for the full tax year, and significantly lower operational workload compared to creating a new PERNR for each cross-company transfer.

Source: SAP Note 3124029 (BAdI for same PERNR, v4 Nov 2025); SAP Note 1902797 (HR Authorization period of responsibility, v7 Sep 2025); SAP Note 3749752 (SSO same PERNR); SAP Help Portal — Implementing ECP (1H 2026); KBA 2536159 (RP 221 constraint)

Business Requirement

Requested Enhancement

entry.title: Customer Returns (BKP) – Create Credit Memo Request Only After Goods Receipt
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement from business in the Customer Returns (BKP) scope item in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, and I would appreciate your guidance on whether this can be achieved using standard SAP functionality/configuration.

Business Scenario

We receive empty dunnage/packaging material from the customer and process the return using the standard Customer Returns process.

Currently, when we create the Returns Order, the following are maintained:

  • Refund Control: Credit Memo Request

As a result, the Credit Memo Request is created automatically before the Goods Receipt (GR) of the returned goods is posted.

Current Process

Returns Order
→ Credit Memo Request created automatically
→ Returns Delivery
→ Goods Receipt

Required Process

Returns Order
→ Returns Delivery
→ Goods Receipt completed successfully
→ Credit Memo Request created

The business requirement is that the customer should be credited only after the returned goods have been physically received and the Goods Receipt has been successfully posted.

Important Constraint

We cannot use Refund Control = Decide Later or No Refund because, in our scenario, these options change the Requirement Type in the Procurement tab to MSR.

We need to retain the current Refund Control setting, as changing it impacts the procurement requirement type and does not meet our business requirement.

Question

Is there any standard configuration or standard functionality in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that allows us to:

  • Retain Refund Control = Credit Memo Request

  • Create the Credit Memo Request only after the Goods Receipt is successfully completed

Any guidance or confirmation from experts would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: New Course: Collaborating with Suppliers and Business Partners with SAP IPD
entry.content:
New Self-Paced Course: Collaborating with Suppliers and Business Partners with SAP Integrated Product Development

Collaboration across company boundaries is essential for successful product development. The new self-paced SAP Learning course, “Collaborating with Suppliers and Business Partners with SAP Integrated Product Development”, helps consultants understand how SAP Integrated Product Development (SAP IPD) enables secure and efficient collaboration with suppliers and external partners throughout the product lifecycle.

In this course, you'll learn how to create collaboration workspaces, manage and share documents, track changes, collaborate on product data, and support decision-making through workflows. The course also highlights how SAP IPD helps increase productivity, improve communication, reduce errors, and ensure stakeholders always work with the latest product information. 

A great learning opportunity for SAP consultants who want to deepen their understanding of collaborative product development and supplier integration scenarios with SAP IPD. 

👉 start learning today: Collaborating with Suppliers and Business Partners with SAP Integrated Product Development

#SAPLearning #SAPIPD #PLM #ProductDevelopment #SupplyChainCollaboration #SAPCommunity

entry.title: Experience with Joule and Claude Code for SAP Datasphere & SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I would like to hear from anyone who has hands-on experience using Joule and/or Claude Code in SAP environments, particularly with SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

I'm interested in understanding:

  • What are the main advantages of Joule and Claude Code when working with SAP Datasphere and SAC?
  • In which scenarios have you found them most useful?
  • What are the limitations or challenges you have encountered?
  • How well do they support development, data modeling, SQL generation, troubleshooting, documentation, and analytics use cases?
  • Have you noticed any significant differences in productivity, accuracy, or integration with SAP tools?
  • If you have used both, which one do you prefer and why?

Any practical examples, lessons learned, or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

TomcatSLDDataSupplierWEB (sap.com~TomcatSLDDataSupplierWEB.war) from SAP note DIGITS

  • SLD Data Supplier can automatically discover and report deployed application to SLD. To be found by the SLD-DS application has to be delivered with a SAP “metadata” XML file which describes software component(s) implemented by the application.

02/07/DIGITS  10:31 p. m.             1,064 SAP_metadata.xml

               1 archivos          1,064 bytes

     Total de archivos en la lista:

 

In the document Apache_Tomcat_SLD-DS_20250601.pdf, section 3.1, Install SLDREG – executable,

What would be the criteria for creating the .CFG and .KEY files?

In point 3.3.1 Configuration of the SLD-DS, what parameters should we include in the sldDataSupplier.properties.template or sldDataSupplier.properties file? Initially, it would be those that appear by default.

What would be the criteria for defining the parameters for BODS 4.3?

 

In the file actol_mini\readme.txt in point 3.

C:\Users\jdominguez25\Downloads\actool_mini\actool_mini>

3. run ACTool by

Windows: run_actool.bat

Y en ACTool User Guide-2.pdf en punto 5.8

5.8 Run ACTool with Interactive User Input

For all steps, you need to run ACTool as root user or sudo on UNIX, or as the administrator role in Windows.

Windows run_actool.bat.

 

The next step is to run run_actool.bat, but upon reviewing the script, we find some instructions (in black) related to configuring and restarting Data Services. This raises the question: Did the application configuration change, requiring a restart?

Is confirmation required to determine if the configuration changed and a restart is necessary to request permission from the client or application owner?

 

@echo off

setlocal enabledelayedexpansion

if exist downloads\sap.com-TomcatSLDDataSupplierWEB-*.war (

if NOT exist downloads\sap.com~TomcatSLDDataSupplierWEB.war (

              cd downloads

              rename sap.com-TomcatSLDDataSupplierWEB-*.war sap.com~TomcatSLDDataSupplierWEB.war

              cd ..

)

)

reg query "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\SAP BusinessObjects\Suite XI 4.0\Installer\PERSON_NAME" /v path  |findstr path >./tmp.txt

if errorlevel 1 (

reg query "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\SAP BusinessObjects\Suite XI 4.0\Installer\LTS" /v path  |findstr path >./tmp.txt

)

set /p myvar= < tmp.txt

echo %myvar:~22%>tmp.txt

set /p BOEDIR= < tmp.txt

del tmp.txt

rem set BOEDIR=C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects

if NOT "%BOEDIR%" == "" (

rem call cpbosdk.bat "%BOEDIR%"

set "CESDK=%BOEDIR%\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\java\lib\cesdk.jar"

)

type conf\ds.properties |findstr /b ds\.service\.name >tmp.txt

set /p myvar= < tmp.txt

echo %myvar:~18%>tmp.txt

set /p DS_SERVICE_NAME= < tmp.txt

del tmp.txt

rem echo DS_SERVICE_NAME=%DS_SERVICE_NAME%

 

 

type conf\ds.properties |findstr /b ds\.restart >tmp.txt

set /p myvar= < tmp.txt

echo %myvar:~13%>tmp.txt

set /p DS_RESTART= < tmp.txt

del tmp.txt

rem echo DS_RESTART='%DS_RESTART%'

 

type conf\boe.properties |findstr /b cms\.ssl >tmp.txt

set /p myvar= < tmp.txt

echo %myvar:~10%>tmp.txt

set /p CMSSSL= < tmp.txt

del tmp.txt

set BOEJVMBIN=%BOEDIR%\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\win64_x64\sapjvm\bin

 

if NOT exist "%BOEJVMBIN%\java.exe" (set BOEJVMBIN=C:\Windows\System32)

rem For Standalone Tomcat, uncomment below line and adapt your JDK

rem set BOEJVMBIN=C:\jdk1.7.0_25\bin

 

set BOEJVMBIN2=%BOEDIR%\Analytics\win64_x64\sapjvm\bin

echo %BOEJVMBIN2%>tmp.txt

if NOT exist "%BOEJVMBIN%\java.exe" (set /p BOEJVMBIN= < tmp.txt)

del tmp.txt

 

if NOT exist "%BOEJVMBIN%\java.exe" (echo no JVM found, please set this variable BOEJVMBIN manually! && GOTO:eof)

echo using JVM=%BOEJVMBIN%

 

set myvar=

rem for /r %%i In (AutoConfigure_lib\*.jar sap_actool*.jar) DO set myvar=!myvar!%%i;

for /r %%i In (sap_actool*.jar) DO set myvar=!myvar!%%i;

rem echo %myvar%

 

set CLASSPATH=.;%myvar%

 

rem set JAVA_OPT=-Xrunjdwp:transport=dt_socket,address=DIGITS,server=y,suspend=n

set JAVA_OPT=%JAVA_OPT% -Dtracelog.logdir=logs -Dtracelog.configfile=BO_trace.ini -Dtracelog.name=ac

set JAVA_OPT=%JAVA_OPT% -Djava.library.path="%BOEDIR%\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\win64_x64"

rem echo JAVA_OPT=%JAVA_OPT%

rem below for CMS SSL

if "%CMSSSL%" == "true" (

set JAVA_OPT=%JAVA_OPT% -Dbusinessobjects.orb.oci.protocol=ssl -DcertDir="%BOEDIR%\SSL" -DtrustedCert=cacert.der -DsslCert=servercert.der -DsslKey=server.key -Dpassphrase=passphrase.txt -Dpsecert=cert.pse

)

if NOT "%CESDK%" == "" (

set "CLASSPATH=%CLASSPATH%;%CESDK%"

echo Using unique BOSDK lib:"%CESDK%"

)

rem echo CLASSPATH=%CLASSPATH%

"%BOEJVMBIN%\java" %JAVA_OPT% com.sap.ac.entry.ConsoleModeLauncher %*

 

if "%DS_RESTART%" == "true" (

if NOT "%DS_SERVICE_NAME%" == "" (

sc stop %DS_SERVICE_NAME%

sc start %DS_SERVICE_NAME%

)

)

 

We need your support to address these questions or concerns so we can make the necessary changes to integrate this development environment into FOCUSED RUN's monitoring system.

 

If you believe we should change the order of the steps or activities to be performed, please also confirm, as we need to integrate the quality assurance and production environments into FOCUSED RUN's monitoring system.

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Celebrate Your SAP Academic Community Conference experience: Earn Your Exclusive Conference Badg
entry.content:
Just a few days after celebrating my 30-year anniversary at SAP, I had the privilege of attending my first SAP Academic Community Conference North America at the University of California, Irvine – Paul Merage School of Business.

While I shared my reflections on LinkedIn, I also wanted to take a moment to thank this incredible community directly.

To the SAP University Alliances team, the faculty members, and my SAP colleagues—thank you for welcoming me into such an inspiring network.

As someone who has spent three decades at SAP while also serving as an Adjunct Faculty Member at West Chester University of Pennsylvania, this conference brought together two passions that have shaped my career: enterprise technology and higher education.

It was an honor to present alongside Isadora Royer and Ana Luiza Treichel Vianna on the SAP Campus Ambassador Program, showcasing how our SAP colleagues can partner with universities to mentor students, collaborate with faculty, and help prepare the next generation of business and technology leaders.

What I will remember most, however, is the people.

I met faculty members from across North America who are deeply committed to preparing students for an AI-enabled future. I experienced firsthand the passion and dedication of the SAP University Alliances team, whose educational resources and programs make a real difference in classrooms around the world. And I was welcomed into a community where many members have been collaborating, learning from one another, and building lasting friendships for more than 15 years.

The keynote sessions, customer and partner presentations, and conversations throughout the conference reinforced something I have always believed: the future of innovation depends on strong partnerships between academia and industry.

A special thank you to Evan Sevits and the entire SAP University Alliances North America team for the invitation, the opportunity to present, and for organizing such an outstanding event.

I leave this conference inspired, with new friendships, new ideas, and an even stronger commitment to supporting our academic community and helping students connect classroom learning with real-world impact.

Thank you for making a first-time attendee feel like part of the family.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 - Introduction...
entry.content:
Dear @rince_reji,

Question 1.

Yes. SAP states that the automatic conversion and assignment apply to all customers during the upgrade to release 2608. Public Cloud supports both 2-system and 3-system landscapes and SAP does not document any landscape-specific exception for this conversion. Therefore the conversion also applies to customers operating in a 2-System Landscape

Question 2.

For non-pilot customers, SAP does not document any mandatory manual reassignment or post-upgrade correction activity. The profiles are assigned automatically through Silent Data Migration. Existing depreciation keys remain unchanged and the depreciation calculation is not affected. After the upgrade the converted depreciation profiles are displayed in the Valuation section of the asset master record next to the depreciation keys. Non-pilot customers can see them but cannot use them. This is a visible UI change without functional impact, and it is worth informing your asset accounting users about it in advance

Pilot customers must additionally recalculate depreciation and review processes or validation rules that refer directly to depreciation keys

Question 3.

For depreciation keys that already exist at the time of the upgrade, SAP documents no exception. All existing depreciation keys are converted during the upgrade to 2608 and the previous depreciation keys are retained

There is one documented limitation and it concerns exactly the case you are asking about. On-the-fly conversion of changed or newly created depreciation keys is listed under a later release, not under 2608. SAP names expert configuration and the scoping of a new country as the examples. A depreciation key created or changed through expert configuration after the 2608 upgrade is therefore not covered by the documented on-the-fly conversion mechanism in release 2608

For non-pilot customers this has no practical effect, since depreciation continues to be calculated using the depreciation key. This becomes relevant mainly for pilot customers if a depreciation key is created or changed after the upgrade, for example through expert configuration or after scoping a new country

More details below:

SAP Help – Transition from Depreciation Keys to Depreciation Profiles

SAP Help System Landscapes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Summary:

For non-pilot 2SL customers the conversion and the assignment to asset master records are automatic. SAP does not document any mandatory manual activity and no exception is documented for SAP-delivered or expert-configured depreciation keys that already exist at the time of the upgrade. The only documented limitation concerns depreciation keys created or changed after the upgrade as described under question 3

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Executing Custom ABAP Programs in SAP BW Bridge Process Chains Using Custom Process Types
entry.content:
Executing Custom ABAP Programs in SAP BW Bridge Process Chains Using Custom Process Types

Introduction

One of the common questions while working with SAP Datasphere, BW Bridge is how to execute custom ABAP programs as part of a Process Chain. Developers familiar with SAP BW or SAP BW/4HANA are accustomed to executing ABAP programs directly from Process Chains (shown below) for validations, prerequisite checks, or custom processing.

SAP BW Bridge provides a supported extensibility framework through Custom Process Types, allowing developers to integrate customer-specific ABAP logic into Process Chains. By implementing a simple ABAP class and registering it as a Custom Process Type, the Process Chain framework can execute custom logic as part of its normal execution flow.

In this blog, we'll walk through the complete implementation using a simple business scenario. We'll develop the ABAP implementation, register it as a Custom Process Type, and execute it within a Process Chain.

Business Scenario

Let's assume you have two dependent Process Chains.

The first Process Chain loads and activates data into an ADSO. The second Process Chain performs downstream activities such as report generation or additional data processing.

Before starting the second Process Chain, we want to verify that today's ADSO activation completed successfully. If the validation succeeds, the Process Chain continues as expected. Otherwise, it stops and informs the administrator that the prerequisite load has not completed successfully.

We'll implement this validation by executing a custom ABAP program through a Custom Process Type.

Solution Overview:

The following diagram illustrates the overall solution.

When the Process Chain reaches the Custom Process Type, the BW Bridge framework invokes the ABAP implementation class. The class executes the custom validation logic and returns the execution status back to the Process Chain. Depending on the returned status, the Process Chain either continues with the next step or follows its configured error path.

Developing the ABAP Implementation:

Every Custom Process Type is backed by an ABAP implementation class. Create a new global ABAP class with the following properties.

Property

Value

Class

ZCL_CPT_ABAP1

Superclass

CL_RSPC_SIMPLE_PROCESS_TYPE

Interface

IF_RSPC_SIMPLE_PROCESS_TYPE

The implementation consists of two methods.

  • CLASS_CONSTRUCTOR – Registers the implementation class with the Custom Process Type framework.
  • EXECUTE – Contains the custom ABAP logic that is executed during the Process Chain run.

The CLASS_CONSTRUCTOR method associates the technical process type with the implementing ABAP class. Ensure the values assigned to c_type and c_class match the values maintained later while creating the Custom Process Type.

  METHOD class_constructor.

    c_type = 'ZCPT_ABAP1'.

    c_class = 'ZCL_CPT_ABAP1'.

  ENDMETHOD.

The EXECUTE method contains the business logic executed by the Process Chain.

For this example, the implementation validates whether the ADSO activation completed successfully on the current day by reading the Process Chain execution log (RSPCPROCESSLOG).

 "Get current system date

 DATA(lv_date) = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ).

 " Get details of the ADSO activation today

 SELECT *

   FROM rspcprocesslog

   WHERE type = 'ADSOACT'

   AND state = 'G'

   AND batchdate = @LV_date

   AND variante = 'ZAKADSO2ACT' " ADSO Activation variant name

   INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_reccount).

If the validation succeeds, the process returns a successful status. Otherwise, it returns a failure status together with an informative runtime message displayed in the Process Chain monitor.

The execution status is controlled using the E_FAILED parameter.

e_failed = ' '.     " Set process step status to GREEN

e_failed = 'X'.     " Set process step status to RED

Messages returned through ET_MESSAGE are displayed directly in the Process Chain monitor, making it easy for administrators to understand the execution result.

The complete implementation is shown below.

Creating the Custom Process Type

After activating the implementation class, open the BW Bridge Cockpit and navigate to Custom Process Types.

Choose Create and maintain the required details.

After saving, the Custom Process Type becomes available in the Process Chain editor.

Creating the Process Chain:

Create a new process chain, provide the required technical name and description.

Insert the newly created Process Variant. Connect it to the appropriate predecessor and successor steps. The Custom Process Type now behaves like any standard Process Chain step and participates in the normal execution flow.

Executing the Process Chain:

Failed Execution

If no successful ADSO activation is found, the validation fails and the Process Chain follows its configured error path.

The following message is displayed in the Process Chain log.

Data NOT loaded to ADSO ZAKADSO2 today. Please verify the prerequisite Process Chain.

Successful Execution

If the ADSO activation is found for the current day, the validation succeeds and the Process Chain continues with the subsequent steps. The following message is displayed in the Process Chain log.

Data successfully loaded to ADSO ZAKADSO2 today.

Conclusion

Custom Process Types provide a flexible and SAP-supported way to execute customer-specific ABAP programs within SAP BW Bridge Process Chains.

In this blog, we developed an ABAP implementation class, registered it as a Custom Process Type, and incorporated it into a Process Chain to validate a prerequisite ADSO activation. Although the example demonstrates a simple dummy validation scenario, the same implementation pattern can be adapted to execute a wide range of customer-specific ABAP programs within the BW Bridge Process Chain framework.

Navigating complex software documentation can be challenging, especially when a product offers as many capabilities as SAP Integration Suite. After many years of growth, that's why our User Assistance team for SAP Integration Suite decided it was time for a change.

We, that is, the authors of the help documents for SAP Integration Suite, have revamped the documentation structure. You'll now find a streamlined main hub that connects you directly to individual capability guides. Simply click the capability name to jump off to the area you need – whether that's API Management, Integration Advisor, or any other capability of SAP Integration Suite.

Curious? Get exploring right away: SAP Integration Suite

GIF showing navigation from Integration Suite to Trading Partner Management guide

 

 

 

 

 

 

What This Means For You

  • Slimmer guides with flatter hierarchies - less scrolling and searching
  • Faster navigation gets you to the right information quickly
  • Focused content shows only what's relevant to your current task

... all with the same quality and surrounded by the modern look of our SAP Help Portal. 🌟

Take Action

By the way, are you already collaboration with us? Thanks to our Open Documentation collaboration process, you can give feedback and raise issues about the documentation using GitHub. Learn more about it in under Collaborate with us on the Migration Guide for SAP Process Orchestration!

Happy exploring!

entry.title: Re: How to expose Supplier confirmation status (LFA1-CONF...
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

Supplier Change-Confirmation Status

Unfortunately, there is no released (C1) CDS view or OData API that directly exposes the fields CONFS, UPDAT, and UPTIM from the LFA1 table in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Since LFA1 is not released for direct access, you cannot select it in a custom CDS view or expose these fields via an extension field.

Alternative Solutions

  1. Use the "Confirm Supplier List" Fiori App (Process-Level Workaround) Rather than reading the confirmation status programmatically, leverage the standard Confirm Supplier List app to manage and monitor pending dual-control confirmations. This app surfaces all suppliers with sensitive-field changes awaiting confirmation, configured via SSCUI 102853 (Define Sensitive Fields for Dual Control – Vendors). [4] [5] While this does not expose CONFS/UPDAT/UPTIM to an external system, it covers the operational use case within the SAP UI.

  2. Custom Field and Logic: You might consider introducing a custom field and updating it using a BAdI implementation or custom logic. This approach would involve capturing the necessary data during the confirmation process and storing it in a custom field.

  3. SAP Influence Program: If the required CDS view is not available, you can submit a request through the SAP Influence Program (at https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/influence-adopt) to have the view released or to have the necessary fields exposed in a future update.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud (C4C) 2608 Pre-Release Briefing
entry.content:
Hi All,

SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud (C4C) Pre-Release Briefing presentation for the upcoming 2608 (Aug 2026) release is now available. We have some key innovations to be shared as part of SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud 2608 Release.

Platform & Integration:

Transition Tools:

  • Data Transfer Tool (DTT) | Onboarding Utilities objects data transfer from V1 to V2 - You can now onboard Utilities objects (Premise, Contract Account) in DTT to transition from V1 to V2.
  • Data Transfer Tool (DTT) | Transfer file size increase in Sales Orders and Sales Quotes - Increase in the Maximum File size limit from 4 MB to 10 MB for Sales Orders and Sales Quotes
  • Data Transfer Tool (DTT) | Introducing ‘Count Summary’ for Surveys - Count Summary is added in Survey Design and Survey Response Objects in DTT
  • Readiness Check Tool (RCT) | Hierarchical display of integration scenarios artifacts - Visual improvements in “Integration Scenarios” tab of RCT to show communication arrangements under communication system they belong to

Feeds | Integration with SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition - As an Administrator, you have an option to use SAP Jam (until 31st December 2026 due to deprecation of SAP Jam collaboration) or SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition for collaboration via Feeds. This was communicated earlier in the SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud 2511 Release Briefing. Kindly plan to move from SAP Jam and start using SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition for collaboration via Feeds based on your requirements.

SAP Service Cloud:

  • Ability to display confirmation pop-up while discarding an email draft from the Timeline tab in Tickets - Now, a confirmation pop-up is displayed when an email draft is discarded on choosing the Discard Draft action from the Timeline tab of Tickets.
  • Enabled Read access (PSM release) for BTD Reference association in the Maintenance Plan BO

SAP Agent Desktop Add-On:

  • Agent Desktop Add-On | CTI Overlay Auto-Minimize and Auto-Popup on Call Events - When an agent accepts an incoming call, the CTI Overlay automatically minimizes so the agent can focus fully on the Agent Desktop. When the customer ends the call, the CTI Overlay pops up automatically — prompting the agent to complete 'Close the Interaction' without any manual clicks.

Thanks,

SAP Sales Cloud & SAP Service Cloud Product Management Team

entry.title: Equipment Summary AI functionality in SAP Field Service Management
entry.content:
SAP Field Service Management has a quiet AI feature that shows the Equipment Summary for technicians has walked into a service job blindly at least once or to give better clarity about the work.

What it's about:

Equipment Summary uses generative AI to write a short summary (maximum 20 number of sentences per summary) of a piece of equipment based on its past activities (maximum of 20 Activities). The Content uses Activity Information, Checklist Instance, Item, Reserved Material, Time Effort, Service Call, Warehouse and User Defined Fields for all objects. The technician gets instant context, the dispatcher gets a clearer basis for assigning the right person. It shows up in Planning and Dispatching (Dispatching Board / Service Calls) and in the mobile app, with links back to the underlying details.

 

How to activate it:

It's a Premium AI feature, so it needs the GENERATIVE_AI role. The role will be assigned at Account Level.

Sections in the Summary:
It is not mandatory to keep all the sections (Flexible for customers):
1. Problem Description
2. Responsible
3. Resolution Steps
4. Root Cause
5. Parts Removed
6. Parts Installed

Advantages:

Faster first-time fixes, less time hunting through history, and smarter dispatch decisions. Very much useful for recurring issues on an equipment.

Limitations:

1. Summary uses activities with 'CLOSED' status. so open work won't appear.
2. Supports every profile language except 'Tamil'.

entry.title: Zum guten Schluss - der Knaller! : Einheit 2 - Video 6
entry.content:
An English version of this lesson is available here.

 

Videolektion "Zum guten Schluss"

In dieser Lektion hast du die Themen und Konzepte der vorherigen beiden Einheiten wiederholt, wie Schleifen, Kostüme und Grafikeffekte, Klone, Nutzerinteraktion und vieles mehr. Dabei hast du ein interaktives Spiel programmiert.

Folge den Schritten aus dem Video in Snap!. Die Aufgaben bauen teilweise auf das Projekt aus dem Video auf. 

 

 

 

Du bist dran

  • Mache dein Spiel ein wenig interessanter, indem du eine Variable hinzufügst, die den prozentualen Anteil der Ballons, die du getroffen hast, anzeigt.
    Für die Berechnung benötigst du mehrere Hilfsvariablen.

    Vergiss nicht, dass du Operatoren ineinander verschachteln kannst, um komplexere Formeln zu bauen.  

 

  • Werde selbst kreativ. Erweitere dein Spiel zum Beispiel um Objekte, die nicht getroffen werden dürfen oder baue etwas komplett anderes in Snap!

 

  • Hier findest du die Aufgaben auch zum Herunterladen:

    • als PDF: https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/rmNznrr?rc=10&doi=SAP1328497 
    • als editierbare docx Version:  https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/58W3Dgi?rc=10&doi=SAP1328496 

    Lösungsvorschläge für Einheit 2 - Lektion 6 findest du in dieser Collection.

 

 

Zusammenfassung - das hast du diese Lektion gelernt

Bausteine dieser Lektion: 

Diese Lektion ist eine Zusammenfassung der informatischen Konzepte aus den ersten beiden Einheiten. Es wurden keine neuen Blöcke verwendet. 

 

Wiederholung

Diese Lektion war eine Zusammenfassung der Inhalte, die du in den vergangenen beiden Einheiten gelernt hast.

Du hast Schleifen verwendet, um Teile deines Skripts wiederholt auszuführen. Mit Zufallswerten hast du die Position und Bewegung deines Objekts bestimmt. Grafikeffekte aus der "Aussehen" Kategorie haben die Farbe und Größe deines Ballons verändert. Außerdem hast du weitere Blöcke aus der "Aussehen" Kategorie verwendet, um zwischen den Kostümen deines Objekts zu wechseln. Das Drehen und Bewegen erfolgte mit Blöcken aus der Kategorie "Bewegung". Mit verschiedenen Startblöcken hast du die entsprechenden Skripte gestartet. Mit Blöcken, die auf Mausklicks reagieren, hast du Benutzerinteraktionen hinzugefügt. Schließlich hast du mithilfe von Klonen mehrere Agenten mit ähnlichem Verhalten zu deinem Spiel hinzugefügt.

Wenn du dein Projekt in der Snap!-Cloud speicherst, kannst du einen Link zum Projekt erstellen und ihn mit deinen Freunden, Eltern, Geschwistern oder anderen Personen teilen. So können sie dein Spiel ebenfalls auf ihrem Smartphone, Tablet oder Computer spielen.

 

Und jetzt?

Das wars mit Einheit 2 - Lektion 6: Zum guten Schluss - der Knaller!

  • Zur nächsten Lektion des Kurses geht es hier: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-young-thinkers-program-blog-posts/mit-daten-arbeiten-eine-kurze-einf%C3%BChrung-in-programmierparadigmen-einheit-3/ba-p/14448216 
  • Zur Übersichtsseite von Einheit 2 geht es hier: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-young-thinkers-program-blog-posts/einheit-2-bausteine-f%C3%BCr-syst... 
  • Zur Übersichtsseite des gesamten Kurses geht es hier: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-young-thinkers-program-blog-posts/programmieren-mit-snap-building-u... 
  • Eine Englische Version des Kurses, bei der ein Zertifikat erworben werden kann, findet sich hier: https://learning.sap.com/courses/get-coding-with-snap-building-up-to-ai 

 

Dieses Material ist veröffentlicht unter der CC 4.0 Lizenz durch das Snap! Team bei SAP

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud EWM: How to Print Only One G...
entry.content:
Hello!

The GR label mechanism in Inventory Management determines the label count from a quantity field. The label type (M7 - GR label) combined with the label form controls which labels print for which goods movement and how many print, and the delivered label forms are quantity-based variants: E1 for the entry quantity, E2 for the quantity in storage, with the equivalents for order quantity and order price quantity.

An alternative is to use the GR slip mechanism: the goods receipt slip output on the material document has an individual slip variant, which is inherently one output per material document item.

  • Clear Label Type and Label Form in the product master. Leaving these blank stops the quantity-driven label generation.
  • Use the individual-slip output type on business object Material Document, not the collective slip. One item > one output.
  • Build a custom form template in Maintain Form Templates, copy the standard GR slip template, resize it to your label stock, strip it down to material / batch / qty / delivery / barcode.
  • Fix the copy count to 1 in Output Parameter Determination. In the BRF+ decision table, NUMBER_OF_COPIES is normally mapped from V_DETERM_PARAMS-NMBROFGRORGISLIPSTOPRINTQTY (the standard rule sets the copies from that field). Replace that mapping with a constant of 1.
  • Result: one label-formatted PDF per material document item.

Make sure: material document items map 1:1 to inbound delivery items only if there's no split. Batch split, split valuation, serial number handling, or a storage-location split at GR will produce multiple material document items for one delivery item, and you'd get a label for each.

References:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/is-it-possible-to-control-number-of-labels-in-goods-receipt-print/qaq-p/12310813

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/goods-receipt-number-of-labels/qaq-p/12333397

entry.title: Asset History Sheet: Standard Key Figure for Total Movements?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am working on an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation for a customer in Portugal.

The customer provided an Excel report with the following fixed asset information by asset class:

  • Opening Acquisition Cost
  • Opening Accumulated Depreciation
  • Opening Net Book Value
  • Additions
  • Retirements
  • Depreciation for the Year
  • Total Movements
  • Closing Acquisition Cost
  • Closing Accumulated Depreciation
  • Closing Net Book Value

After reviewing the standard Asset History Sheet, I was able to map almost all of the requested values to the standard key figures:

Customer report Asset History Sheet Key Figure
Opening Acquisition CostAPC FY Start
Opening Accumulated DepreciationAccumulated Depreciation at FY Start
Opening Net Book ValueNet Book Value FY Start
AdditionsAcquisition, Current Year (+ Post-Capitalization APC, Current Year, if applicable)
RetirementsRetirements, Current Year
Depreciation for the YearDepreciation, Current Year
Closing Acquisition CostAPC at Reporting Date
Closing Accumulated DepreciationAcc. Depr. at Reporting Date
Closing Net Book ValueNet Book Value at Reporting Date

The only value I could not identify is Total Movements.

In the customer's report, this value is calculated as:

Additions (+ Post-Capitalizations, if applicable) − Retirements − Depreciation for the Year

My question is:

  • Is there any standard Key Figure in the Asset History Sheet or I_AssetHistorySheetCube that already provides this value?
  • If not, is there any standard CDS View that exposes this calculation?
  • Or is the recommended Clean Core approach in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to calculate this value externally (for example, using a Custom Analytical Query, SAP Analytics Cloud, or another reporting solution)?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Customer Returns (BKP) – Create Credit Memo Request O...
entry.content:
Dear @Thiru2203,

No. In Public Cloud the requested sequence is not supported while retaining Refund Control = Create Credit Memo Request (P). With Refund Control P the Credit Memo Request is created when the returns order is released. Its creation is not dependent on the subsequent Goods Receipt status

More details below:

SAP Help – Customer Compensation

SAP Best Practices – Customer Returns: Approve/Reject/Rework Returns Order

The standard SAP option for postponing the Credit Memo Request until the returns process has been completed is Refund Control = Decide Later (R)

More details below:

SAP KBA 3433519 – How to avoid credit memo to be created before the end of the returns process?

Therefore if decide later cannot be used because it changes the requirement type to MSR there is no documented standard configuration that both retains Refund Control P and creates the Credit Memo Request only after Goods Receipt

The closest standard alternative is to configure a default billing block for the Credit Memo Request type GA2. The Credit Memo Request would still be created when the returns order is released but the actual Credit Memo could not be created until the Goods Receipt has been verified and the billing block has been removed manually. SAP does not provide a standard automatic dependency that removes the billing block based on the Goods Receipt status

More details below:

SAP Help – Credit Memo Requests

SAP Help – Manage Sales Document Workflows

SAP Help – Manage Credit Memo Requests – Version 2

IMPORTANT: If automatic creation of the Credit Memo Request after Goods Receipt is required while retaining Refund Control P the requirement can be submitted as an improvement request through SAP Continuous Influence

More details below:

SAP Continuous Influence for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud: XBLNR as reference instead of accountin...
entry.content:
Confirm which tree was actually used

To see which mapping the run really applied, open the Map Format Data app and use its Trace function — the Trace button sits above the list on the Outgoing tab, or on the top right of your format's detail screen. The mapping has to be in a released version to be traced: set tracepoints on the relevant nodes, generate a payment file, then open the trace log. It shows node by node whether the engine used your copy or the standard tree. Reference: Trace Outgoing Format Mappings.

For a quicker check, download the generated file in the Manage Payment Media app and look at the output. If it still matches the standard layout, the missing Deviating Format Tree link — or the mapping still being in draft — is probably the cause.

BR
M.
